title: rubia peregrina var. dalmatica
intro: Rubia peregrina var. dalmatica, also known as the Dalmatian or Albanian root, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, used for its medicinal properties and health benefits. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anthraquinone content, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antiseptic, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Rubia peregrina var. dalmatica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
